title.raw: 《淮南衡山列傳》 Biographies of Huainan and Hengshan

sentences.zh: 淮南厲王長者，高祖少子也，其母故趙王張敖美人。

translations.literal: The Fierce King of Huainan, Chang, was the youngest son of Gaozu. His mother had been a beauty of Zhang Ao, the former King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: King Li of Huainan, whose personal name was Chang, was Emperor Gaozu's youngest son. His mother had been a consort of Zhang Ao, the former King of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 高祖八年，從東垣過趙，趙王獻之美人。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Gaozu, when he passed through Zhao from Dongyuan, the King of Zhao presented this beauty to him.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year of Gaozu's reign, the emperor passed through Zhao on his way from Dongyuan, and the King of Zhao presented this consort to him.

sentences.zh: 厲王母得幸焉，有身。

translations.literal: The mother of King Li received his favor and became with child.
translations.idiomatic: The future mother of King Li won the emperor's favor and became pregnant.

sentences.zh: 趙王敖弗敢內宮，為筑外宮而捨之。

translations.literal: King Ao of Zhao did not dare place her in the inner palace, and built an outer palace to house her.
translations.idiomatic: King Ao of Zhao did not dare bring her into his own palace, so he built a separate residence outside the palace walls to lodge her.

sentences.zh: 及貫高等謀反柏人事發覺，并逮治王，盡收捕王母兄弟美人，系之河內。

translations.literal: When the plot of Guan Gao and others to rebel at Bairen was discovered, the king was arrested and tried, and all of the king's mother, brothers, and beauties were seized and imprisoned in Henei.
translations.idiomatic: When the conspiracy of Guan Gao and his accomplices to assassinate the emperor at Bairen was uncovered, King Ao was arrested and put on trial, and all of the king's female relations, brothers, and consorts were seized and imprisoned in Henei.

sentences.zh: 厲王母亦系，告吏曰：「得幸上，有身。」

translations.literal: The mother of King Li was also imprisoned. She told the officials: 'I have received the favor of the Emperor and am with child.'
translations.idiomatic: The mother of King Li was likewise imprisoned. She told the officials, 'I have received the emperor's favor and am carrying his child.'

sentences.zh: 吏以聞上，上方怒趙王，未理厲王母。

translations.literal: The officials reported this to the Emperor, but the Emperor was in a fury at the King of Zhao and did not attend to King Li's mother.
translations.idiomatic: The officials reported this to the emperor, but he was still furious with the King of Zhao and paid no attention to King Li's mother.

sentences.zh: 厲王母弟趙兼因辟陽侯言呂后，呂后妒，弗肯白，辟陽侯不彊爭。

translations.literal: The younger brother of King Li's mother, Zhao Jian, asked the Marquis of Biyang to speak to Empress Lu, but Empress Lu was jealous and refused to report it, and the Marquis of Biyang did not press the matter forcefully.
translations.idiomatic: King Li's maternal uncle, Zhao Jian, asked the Marquis of Biyang to intercede with Empress Lu, but the empress was jealous and refused to bring the matter to the emperor's attention. The Marquis of Biyang did not press the case.

sentences.zh: 及厲王母已生厲王，恚，即自殺。

translations.literal: After King Li's mother had given birth to King Li, in her fury she killed herself.
translations.idiomatic: After King Li's mother gave birth to him, she was so filled with resentment that she took her own life.

sentences.zh: 吏奉厲王詣上，上悔，令呂后母之，而葬厲王母真定。

translations.literal: The officials brought King Li to the Emperor. The Emperor was remorseful and ordered Empress Lu to raise him as a mother, and buried King Li's mother at Zhending.
translations.idiomatic: The officials brought the infant King Li before the emperor. Filled with remorse, the emperor ordered Empress Lu to raise the child as her own and had King Li's mother buried at Zhending.

sentences.zh: 真定，厲王母之家在焉，父世縣也。

translations.literal: Zhending was where King Li's mother's family resided, the county of her ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: Zhending was where King Li's mother's family lived, the county of her forebears.

sentences.zh: 高祖十一年（十）［七］月，淮南王黥布反，立子長為淮南王，王黥布故地，凡四郡。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of Gaozu's eleventh year, when the King of Huainan Qing Bu rebelled, he established his son Chang as King of Huainan, to rule over Qing Bu's former territory, a total of four commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of Gaozu's eleventh year, Qing Bu, the King of Huainan, rose in rebellion. The emperor appointed his son Chang as the new King of Huainan, to rule over Qing Bu's former territory, which comprised four commanderies.

sentences.zh: 上自將兵擊滅布，厲王遂即位。

translations.literal: The Emperor personally led troops to attack and destroy Bu, and King Li thereupon assumed the throne.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor personally led his armies to crush Qing Bu's rebellion, and King Li then ascended to his throne.

sentences.zh: 厲王蚤失母，常附呂后，孝惠、呂后時以故得幸無患害，而常心怨辟陽侯，弗敢發。

translations.literal: King Li had lost his mother early and always attached himself to Empress Lu. During the reigns of Emperor Hui and Empress Lu, he was favored on this account and suffered no harm, but in his heart he always resented the Marquis of Biyang, though he dared not show it.
translations.idiomatic: Having lost his mother early, King Li always kept close to Empress Lu. During the reigns of Emperor Hui and Empress Lu, this attachment kept him safe from harm, though all the while he harbored a deep resentment toward the Marquis of Biyang that he dared not reveal.

sentences.zh: 及孝文帝初即位，淮南王自以為最親，驕蹇，數不奉法。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen first ascended the throne, the King of Huainan considered himself the closest relative, became arrogant and insolent, and repeatedly violated the law.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen first came to the throne, the King of Huainan, considering himself the emperor's closest living relative, grew arrogant and insolent, repeatedly flouting the law.

sentences.zh: 上以親故，常寬赦之。

translations.literal: The Emperor, on account of their kinship, always generously pardoned him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, on account of their close kinship, always showed him leniency and forgave his transgressions.

sentences.zh: 三年，入朝。

translations.literal: In the third year, he came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Emperor Wen's reign, King Li came to the capital to pay court.

sentences.zh: 甚橫。

translations.literal: He was very arrogant.
translations.idiomatic: His behavior was exceedingly overbearing.

sentences.zh: 從上入苑囿獵，與上同車，常謂上「大兄」。

translations.literal: He accompanied the Emperor into the parks to hunt, rode in the same carriage as the Emperor, and always called the Emperor 'Elder Brother.'
translations.idiomatic: He accompanied the emperor on hunts in the imperial parks, rode in the same carriage, and habitually addressed the emperor as 'Elder Brother.'

sentences.zh: 厲王有材力，力能扛鼎，乃往請辟陽侯。

translations.literal: King Li possessed great strength, strong enough to lift a cauldron, and went to request an audience with the Marquis of Biyang.
translations.idiomatic: King Li was a man of tremendous physical strength, powerful enough to lift a bronze tripod. He went to call upon the Marquis of Biyang.

sentences.zh: 辟陽侯出見之，即自袖鐵椎椎辟陽侯，令從者魏敬剄之。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Biyang came out to see him, he drew an iron mallet from his sleeve and struck the Marquis, then ordered his attendant Wei Jing to behead him.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Biyang came out to receive him, King Li drew an iron mallet from his sleeve and struck the marquis down, then ordered his attendant Wei Jing to cut off his head.

sentences.zh: 厲王乃馳走闕下，肉袒謝曰：「臣母不當坐趙事，其時辟陽侯力能得之呂后，弗爭，罪一也。

translations.literal: King Li then rushed to the palace gate, bared his upper body, and apologized, saying: 'Your subject's mother should not have been implicated in the affair of Zhao. At that time, the Marquis of Biyang had the influence to obtain her release from Empress Lu but did not contend for it. This was his first crime.
translations.idiomatic: King Li then rode at full speed to the palace gate, bared his upper body in submission, and declared: 'Your subject's mother should never have been implicated in the affair of Zhao. The Marquis of Biyang had the influence to secure her release from Empress Lu, yet he refused to plead her case. This was his first crime.

sentences.zh: 趙王如意子母無罪，呂後殺之，辟陽侯弗爭，罪二也。

translations.literal: Prince Ruyi of Zhao and his mother were innocent, yet Empress Lu killed them, and the Marquis of Biyang did not contend. This was his second crime.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Ruyi of Zhao and his mother were innocent, yet Empress Lu had them killed, and the Marquis of Biyang did nothing to intervene. This was his second crime.

sentences.zh: 呂后王諸呂，欲以危劉氏，辟陽侯弗爭，罪三也。

translations.literal: Empress Lu made the Lu clan kings, intending to endanger the Liu house, and the Marquis of Biyang did not contend. This was his third crime.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu ennobled members of the Lu clan as kings, threatening the very survival of the House of Liu, and the Marquis of Biyang did nothing to oppose her. This was his third crime.

sentences.zh: 臣謹為天下誅賊臣辟陽侯，報母之仇，謹伏闕下請罪。」

translations.literal: Your subject has respectfully executed the treacherous minister, the Marquis of Biyang, on behalf of the realm, and has avenged his mother. He respectfully prostrates himself below the palace gate to await punishment.'
translations.idiomatic: Your subject has taken it upon himself to execute this treacherous minister on behalf of the empire and to avenge his mother's death. He now prostrates himself before the palace gate and awaits his punishment.'

sentences.zh: 孝文傷其志，為親故，弗治，赦厲王。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen was moved by his resolve, and on account of their kinship, did not punish him but pardoned King Li.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen was moved by the depth of his feeling, and on account of their kinship, chose not to punish him and pardoned King Li.

sentences.zh: 當是時，薄太后及太子諸大臣皆憚厲王，厲王以此歸國益驕恣，不用漢法，出入稱警蹕，稱制，自為法令，擬於天子。

translations.literal: At this time, Empress Dowager Bo, the Crown Prince, and all the great ministers were wary of King Li. King Li, emboldened by this, returned to his kingdom even more arrogant and willful, disregarding Han law, having roads cleared and subjects warned away when he went out, issuing edicts, making his own laws, and conducting himself as though he were the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: At this time, Empress Dowager Bo, the Crown Prince, and all the senior ministers lived in fear of King Li. Emboldened by their deference, King Li returned to his kingdom more arrogant and willful than ever. He disregarded the laws of the Han, had the roads cleared and onlookers driven away when he traveled, issued his own edicts and decrees, and conducted himself in every way as though he were the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 六年，令男子但等七十人與棘蒲侯柴武太子奇謀，以輂車四十乘反谷口，令人使閩越、匈奴。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, he ordered the commoner Dan and seventy others to conspire with Qi, the son of Chai Wu, Marquis of Jipu, to rebel at Gukou with forty war chariots, and sent envoys to Minyue and the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, he ordered a commoner named Dan and seventy others to join in a conspiracy with Qi, the son of Chai Wu, Marquis of Jipu, planning to launch a revolt at Gukou with forty war chariots. He also sent envoys to the kingdoms of Minyue and the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 事覺，治之，使使召淮南王。

translations.literal: The plot was discovered and investigated, and envoys were sent to summon the King of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: The plot was discovered and an investigation launched. Envoys were dispatched to summon the King of Huainan.

sentences.zh: 淮南王至長安。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan arrived at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was brought to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 「丞相臣張倉、典客臣馮敬、行御史大夫事宗正臣逸、廷尉臣賀、備盜賊中尉臣福昧死言：淮南王長廢先帝法，不聽天子詔，居處無度，為黃屋蓋乘輿，出入擬於天子，擅為法令，不用漢法。

translations.literal: 'Your ministers, Chancellor Zhang Cang, Director of Guests Feng Jing, Acting Grandee Secretary and Director of the Imperial Clan Yi, Commandant of Justice He, and Colonel in Charge of Bandit Suppression Fu, risk death to submit: The King of Huainan, Chang, has cast aside the laws of the late emperor, refused to obey the Son of Heaven's edicts, lived without restraint, had a yellow canopy made for his carriage, gone about in imitation of the Son of Heaven, enacted his own laws, and rejected the laws of the Han.
translations.idiomatic: 'Your ministers—Chancellor Zhang Cang, Director of Guests Feng Jing, Acting Grandee Secretary and Director of the Imperial Clan Yi, Commandant of Justice He, and Colonel of Bandit Suppression Fu—risk death to submit the following: The King of Huainan, Chang, has cast aside the laws established by the late emperor, refused to obey the edicts of the Son of Heaven, lived without any sense of propriety, fitted his carriage with a yellow canopy, traveled in the manner of the Son of Heaven, enacted his own laws and decrees, and refused to observe the laws of the Han.

sentences.zh: 及所置吏，以其郎中春為丞相，聚收漢諸侯人及有罪亡者，匿與居，為治家室，賜其財物爵祿田宅，爵或至關內侯，奉以二千石，所不當得，欲以有為。

translations.literal: As for the officials he appointed, he made his Palace Attendant Chun his Chancellor, gathered and sheltered fugitives from the Han vassal states and those guilty of crimes, hid and lived with them, provided them with residences, and bestowed upon them wealth, noble ranks, stipends, and estates. Some were given the rank of Marquis Within the Pass, with salaries of two thousand bushels—all unauthorized—with the intent of carrying out his designs.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed officials of his own choosing, elevating his Palace Attendant Chun to the post of Chancellor. He gathered fugitives from the Han vassal states and men guilty of crimes, sheltered them in his kingdom, provided them with houses, and bestowed upon them wealth, noble ranks, stipends, fields, and residences. Some were even granted the rank of Marquis Within the Pass with salaries of two thousand bushels—all without authorization—in order to serve his ambitions.

sentences.zh: 大夫但、士五開章等七十人與棘蒲侯太子奇謀反，欲以危宗廟社稷。

translations.literal: The Grandee Dan, the commoner Kai Zhang, and seventy others conspired with Qi, the son of the Marquis of Jipu, to rebel, intending to endanger the ancestral temples and the altars of state.
translations.idiomatic: The Grandee Dan, the commoner Kai Zhang, and seventy others conspired with Qi, the son of the Marquis of Jipu, to stage a rebellion that would endanger the ancestral temples and the altars of state.

sentences.zh: 使開章陰告長，與謀使閩越及匈奴發其兵。

translations.literal: They had Kai Zhang secretly inform Chang, and together they plotted to have Minyue and the Xiongnu raise their troops.
translations.idiomatic: Kai Zhang was sent to secretly inform Chang, and together they plotted to persuade the kingdoms of Minyue and the Xiongnu to mobilize their forces.

sentences.zh: 開章之淮南見長，長數與坐語飲食，為家室娶婦，以二千石俸奉之。

translations.literal: Kai Zhang went to Huainan and met with Chang. Chang frequently sat with him to talk and dine, found him a wife and a residence, and gave him a salary of two thousand bushels.
translations.idiomatic: Kai Zhang traveled to Huainan and met with Chang. Chang regularly invited him to sit and dine, arranged a wife and a residence for him, and provided him with a salary of two thousand bushels.

sentences.zh: 開章使人告但，已言之王。

translations.literal: Kai Zhang sent someone to tell Dan that he had already reported the matter to the king.
translations.idiomatic: Kai Zhang sent word to Dan that he had already informed the king of the plot.

sentences.zh: 春使使報但等。

translations.literal: Chun sent messengers to report to Dan and the others.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor Chun dispatched messengers to relay the news to Dan and his associates.

sentences.zh: 吏覺知，使長安尉奇等往捕開章。

translations.literal: The authorities discovered the plot and sent the Chang'an Colonel Qi and others to arrest Kai Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: When the authorities uncovered the plot, they dispatched Colonel Qi of Chang'an and others to arrest Kai Zhang.

sentences.zh: 長匿不予，與故中尉蕑忌謀，殺以閉口。

translations.literal: Chang hid him and refused to hand him over, then conspired with the former Colonel Jian Ji to kill him and silence him.
translations.idiomatic: Chang concealed Kai Zhang and refused to hand him over. He then conspired with the former Colonel Jian Ji to have Kai Zhang killed to silence him.

sentences.zh: 為棺槨衣衾，葬之肥陵邑，謾吏曰『不知安在』。

translations.literal: He provided a coffin, an outer coffin, clothing, and a coverlet, buried him at the town of Feiling, and lied to the officials, saying, "I do not know where he is."
translations.idiomatic: He had a coffin prepared with proper shrouds and burial garments, buried him at the town of Feiling, and lied to the officials, saying, 'I do not know where he is.'

sentences.zh: 又詳聚土，樹表其上，曰『開章死，埋此下』。

translations.literal: He also pretended to pile up earth and plant a marker on top, saying, 'Kai Zhang is dead and buried here.'
translations.idiomatic: He also had a mound of earth heaped up with a marker planted on top as a decoy, claiming, 'Kai Zhang is dead and buried here.'

sentences.zh: 及長身自賊殺無罪者一人；

translations.literal: Furthermore, Chang personally murdered one innocent person;
translations.idiomatic: Furthermore, Chang personally murdered one person who had committed no crime;

sentences.zh: 令吏論殺無罪者六人；

translations.literal: he ordered officials to sentence and kill six innocent persons;
translations.idiomatic: he ordered his officials to condemn and execute six innocent persons;

sentences.zh: 為亡命棄市罪詐捕命者以除罪；

translations.literal: he fraudulently arrested others to substitute for fugitives sentenced to execution in order to cancel their crimes;
translations.idiomatic: he had innocent men seized as substitutes for fugitives sentenced to public execution, thereby fraudulently clearing the fugitives' records;

sentences.zh: 擅罪人，罪人無告劾，系治城旦舂以上十四人；

translations.literal: he arbitrarily convicted people, convicting them without formal accusation, and imprisoned fourteen people with sentences of hard labor or above;
translations.idiomatic: he arbitrarily convicted people without formal charges or legal proceedings, sentencing fourteen of them to hard labor or worse;

sentences.zh: 赦免罪人，死罪十八人，城旦舂以下五十八人；

translations.literal: he pardoned and released criminals: eighteen sentenced to death and fifty-eight sentenced to hard labor or below;
translations.idiomatic: he pardoned criminals on his own authority, including eighteen who had been sentenced to death and fifty-eight sentenced to hard labor or lesser punishments;

sentences.zh: 賜人爵關內侯以下九十四人。

translations.literal: he bestowed noble ranks of Marquis Within the Pass or below upon ninety-four people.
translations.idiomatic: he bestowed noble ranks up to Marquis Within the Pass upon ninety-four people.

sentences.zh: 前日長病，陛下憂苦之，使使者賜書、棗脯。

translations.literal: Previously, when Chang was ill, Your Majesty was worried and pained, and sent envoys with a letter, dates, and dried meats.
translations.idiomatic: Previously, when Chang fell ill, Your Majesty was deeply concerned and sent envoys bearing an imperial letter along with dates and dried meats.

sentences.zh: 長不欲受賜，不肯見拜使者。

translations.literal: Chang did not wish to accept the gifts and refused to receive or bow to the envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Chang refused to accept the gifts and would not deign to receive or bow to the imperial envoys.

sentences.zh: 南海民處廬江界中者反，淮南吏卒擊之。

translations.literal: When the people of Nanhai living within the borders of Lujiang revolted, the officials and soldiers of Huainan attacked them.
translations.idiomatic: When the people of Nanhai who had settled within the borders of Lujiang Commandery rose in revolt, the officials and soldiers of Huainan were sent to suppress them.

sentences.zh: 陛下以淮南民貧苦，遣使者賜長帛五千匹，以賜吏卒勞苦者。

translations.literal: Your Majesty, taking pity on the poverty and hardship of the people of Huainan, sent envoys to bestow five thousand bolts of silk upon Chang, to be distributed to the officials and soldiers who had toiled and suffered.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty, taking pity on the poverty and hardship of the people of Huainan, sent envoys bearing five thousand bolts of silk for Chang to distribute among the officials and soldiers who had endured such toil.

sentences.zh: 長不欲受賜，謾言曰『無勞苦者』。

translations.literal: Chang did not wish to accept the gifts and deceitfully said, 'There are no one who has toiled.'
translations.idiomatic: Chang refused to accept the gifts and lied, saying, 'There is no one here who has toiled or suffered.'

sentences.zh: 南海民王織上書獻璧皇帝，忌擅燔其書，不以聞。

translations.literal: The man of Nanhai, Wang Zhi, submitted a memorial offering a jade disc to the Emperor. Jian Ji on his own authority burned the memorial and did not report it.
translations.idiomatic: A man of Nanhai named Wang Zhi submitted a memorial offering a jade disc to the emperor. Jian Ji burned the memorial of his own accord and never reported the matter.

sentences.zh: 吏請召治忌，長不遣，謾言曰『忌病』。

translations.literal: The officials requested that Ji be summoned and tried, but Chang would not send him, and deceitfully said, 'Ji is ill.'
translations.idiomatic: When the officials requested that Jian Ji be summoned and tried, Chang refused to hand him over and lied, saying, 'Ji is ill.'

sentences.zh: 春又請長，願入見，長怒曰『女欲離我自附漢』。

translations.literal: Chun again asked Chang, wishing to go to see the Emperor. Chang angrily said, 'You wish to leave me and attach yourself to the Han!'
translations.idiomatic: When the Chancellor Chun again asked for permission to travel to the capital for an audience with the emperor, Chang flew into a rage, saying, 'You wish to abandon me and align yourself with the Han!'

sentences.zh: 長當棄市，臣請論如法。」

translations.literal: Chang deserves to be executed in the marketplace. Your ministers request that he be judged according to the law.'
translations.idiomatic: Chang deserves public execution. Your ministers request that he be sentenced in accordance with the law.'

sentences.zh: 制曰：「朕不忍致法於王，其與列侯二千石議。」

translations.literal: The imperial edict said: 'We cannot bear to impose the law upon the King. Let the marquises and officials of two thousand bushels discuss the matter.'
translations.idiomatic: The imperial edict replied: 'We cannot bear to impose the full force of the law upon the King. Let the marquises and officials of two-thousand-bushel rank deliberate on the matter.'

sentences.zh: 「臣倉、臣敬、臣逸、臣福、臣賀昧死言：臣謹與列侯吏二千石臣嬰等四十三人議，皆曰『長不奉法度，不聽天子詔，乃陰聚徒黨及謀反者，厚養亡命，欲以有為』。

translations.literal: 'Your ministers Cang, Jing, Yi, Fu, and He risk death to submit: Your ministers have carefully deliberated with the marquises and officials of two thousand bushels, including Ying and forty-three others, who all say, "Chang has not upheld the laws and regulations, has not obeyed the edicts of the Son of Heaven, has secretly gathered followers and conspirators, lavishly supported fugitives, and intended to carry out his designs."
translations.idiomatic: 'Your ministers Cang, Jing, Yi, Fu, and He risk death to submit the following: We have carefully deliberated with the marquises and officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, together with Ying and forty-three others, who unanimously declare: "Chang has refused to uphold the laws and regulations, has disobeyed the edicts of the Son of Heaven, has secretly gathered a faction of followers and conspirators, has lavishly supported fugitives, and has intended to carry out his designs."'

sentences.zh: 臣等議論如法。」

translations.literal: Your ministers recommend sentencing according to the law.'
translations.idiomatic: We, your ministers, recommend that he be sentenced according to the law.'

sentences.zh: 制曰：「朕不忍致法於王，其赦長死罪，廢勿王。」

translations.literal: The imperial edict said: 'We cannot bear to impose the law upon the King. Let Chang be pardoned of the death penalty and deposed from his kingship.'
translations.idiomatic: The imperial edict replied: 'We cannot bear to impose the full force of the law upon the King. Let Chang be spared the death penalty but stripped of his kingship.'

sentences.zh: 「臣倉等昧死言：長有大死罪，陛下不忍致法，幸赦，廢勿王。

translations.literal: 'Your ministers Cang and the others risk death to submit: Chang has committed a great crime deserving death. Your Majesty could not bear to impose the law and graciously pardoned him, deposing him from kingship.
translations.idiomatic: 'Your ministers Cang and the others risk death to submit the following: Chang has committed crimes deserving of death. Your Majesty could not bear to impose the full penalty and graciously pardoned him, stripping him of his kingship.

sentences.zh: 臣請處蜀郡嚴道邛郵，遣其子母從居，縣為筑蓋家室，皆廩食給薪菜鹽豉炊食器席蓐。

translations.literal: Your ministers request that he be placed at the Qiong postal station in Yandao County of Shu Commandery, that his children and their mothers be sent to live with him, that the county build a covered residence for them, and that they be supplied with grain, firewood, vegetables, salt, fermented beans, cooking utensils, mats, and bedding.
translations.idiomatic: We request that he be sent to the Qiong postal station in Yandao County of Shu Commandery, that his children and their mothers be permitted to accompany him, that the county build a residence for them, and that they be provided with grain rations, firewood, vegetables, salt, fermented beans, cooking utensils, mats, and bedding.

sentences.zh: 臣等昧死請，請布告天下。」

translations.literal: Your ministers risk death to make this request and ask that it be proclaimed throughout the realm.'
translations.idiomatic: We, your ministers, risk death to make this request and ask that the decision be proclaimed throughout the empire.'

sentences.zh: 制曰：「計食長給肉日五斤，酒二斗。

translations.literal: The imperial edict said: 'In addition to the food rations, Chang shall be provided with five catties of meat per day and two pecks of wine.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial edict replied: 'In addition to the standard rations, Chang shall be provided with five catties of meat per day and two pecks of wine.

sentences.zh: 令故美人才人得幸者十人從居。

translations.literal: Let ten of his former beauties and talented women who were favored accompany him.
translations.idiomatic: Let ten of his former consorts whom he favored accompany him.

sentences.zh: 他可。」

translations.literal: The rest is approved.'
translations.idiomatic: As to the rest, it is approved.'

sentences.zh: 盡誅所與謀者。

translations.literal: All those who had conspired with him were executed.
translations.idiomatic: All those who had taken part in the conspiracy were put to death.

sentences.zh: 於是乃遣淮南王，載以輜車，令縣以次傳。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Huainan was sent away, transported in a covered cart, and passed from county to county in succession.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was then sent into exile, transported in a covered cart, and passed from one county to the next along the route.

sentences.zh: 是時袁盎諫上曰：「上素驕淮南王，弗為置嚴傅相，以故至此。

translations.literal: At this time Yuan Ang remonstrated with the Emperor, saying: 'Your Majesty has always indulged the King of Huainan and never appointed strict tutors or ministers for him. This is why he has come to this.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Yuan Ang remonstrated with the emperor, saying, 'Your Majesty has always indulged the King of Huainan and never appointed stern tutors or counselors to guide him. This is how matters came to this pass.

sentences.zh: 且淮南王為人剛，今暴摧折之。

translations.literal: Moreover, the King of Huainan is a man of unyielding temperament, and now you have abruptly crushed him.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan is a man of fierce and unyielding temperament, and now you have broken him so suddenly.

sentences.zh: 臣恐卒逢霧露病死。

translations.literal: Your minister fears that he may suddenly encounter fog and dew and die of illness.
translations.idiomatic: I fear he may fall ill from exposure along the road and die.

sentences.zh: 陛下為有殺弟之名，柰何！」

translations.literal: Your Majesty would then bear the name of having killed his own brother. What then!'
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty would then bear the reputation of having killed his own brother. How could that be endured!'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾特苦之耳，今復之。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'I am merely making him suffer for a time. I will soon restore him.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied, 'I only mean to make him suffer for a while. I will restore him before long.'

sentences.zh: 縣傳淮南王者皆不敢發車封。

translations.literal: The counties that transported the King of Huainan all dared not open the seals on his cart.
translations.idiomatic: The counties responsible for transporting the King of Huainan along the route all feared to break the seals on his cart.

sentences.zh: 淮南王乃謂侍者曰：「誰謂乃公勇者？

translations.literal: The King of Huainan then said to his attendants: 'Who used to call your lord brave?
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan said to his attendants, 'Who was it that called your lord a brave man?

sentences.zh: 吾安能勇！

translations.literal: How can I be brave!
translations.idiomatic: How could I ever have been called brave!

sentences.zh: 吾以驕故不聞吾過至此。

translations.literal: It was because of my arrogance that I never heard of my faults and have come to this.
translations.idiomatic: It was my own arrogance that kept me from hearing of my faults, and so I have come to this end.

sentences.zh: 人生一世閒，安能邑邑如此！」

translations.literal: A man lives but one life in this world. How can I go on in such sorrow!'
translations.idiomatic: A man has but one life in this world. How can I bear to live in such misery!'

sentences.zh: 乃不食死。

translations.literal: He then refused to eat and died.
translations.idiomatic: He refused all food and starved himself to death.

sentences.zh: 至雍，雍令發封，以死聞。

translations.literal: When the cart reached Yong, the magistrate of Yong opened the seals and reported the death.
translations.idiomatic: When the cart reached Yong, the magistrate broke the seals and discovered the king was dead. He reported the death to the throne.

sentences.zh: 上哭甚悲，謂袁盎曰：「吾不聽公言，卒亡淮南王。」

translations.literal: The Emperor wept bitterly and said to Yuan Ang: 'I did not heed your words, and in the end I have lost the King of Huainan.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wept bitterly and said to Yuan Ang, 'I did not heed your counsel, and in the end I have lost the King of Huainan.'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「不可柰何，願陛下自寬。」

translations.literal: Ang said: 'Nothing can be done about it now. I hope Your Majesty will take comfort.'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang replied, 'Nothing can be done about it now. I beg Your Majesty to ease your grief.'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「為之柰何？」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'What shall be done about it?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, 'What is to be done?'

sentences.zh: 盎曰：「獨斬丞相、御史以謝天下乃可。」

translations.literal: Ang said: 'Only by beheading the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary to apologize to the realm will it suffice.'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Ang replied, 'Only by executing the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary as an apology to the empire will it suffice.'

sentences.zh: 上即令丞相、御史逮考諸縣傳送淮南王不發封餽侍者，皆棄市。

translations.literal: The Emperor immediately ordered the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary to arrest and interrogate the officials of the counties that had transported the King of Huainan without breaking the seals or providing him with food and attendants. All were executed publicly.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor immediately ordered the Chancellor and the Grandee Secretary to arrest and interrogate the officials of every county along the route who had failed to break the seals on the cart or provide the king with food and attendants. All of them were publicly executed.

sentences.zh: 乃以列侯葬淮南王於雍，守冢三十戶。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan was then buried at Yong with the rites of a marquis, and thirty households were assigned to tend his tomb.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was then buried at Yong with the rites befitting a marquis, and thirty households were assigned to tend his tomb.

sentences.zh: 孝文八年，上憐淮南王，淮南王有子四人，皆七八歲，乃封子安為阜陵侯，子勃為安陽侯，子賜為陽周侯，子良為東成侯。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Emperor Wen, the Emperor pitied the King of Huainan. The King of Huainan had four sons, all seven or eight years old, and so he enfeoffed his son An as Marquis of Fuling, his son Bo as Marquis of Anyang, his son Ci as Marquis of Yangzhou, and his son Liang as Marquis of Dongcheng.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year of Emperor Wen's reign, the emperor took pity on the memory of the King of Huainan. As the late king had left four sons, all seven or eight years old, the emperor enfeoffed An as Marquis of Fuling, Bo as Marquis of Anyang, Ci as Marquis of Yangzhou, and Liang as Marquis of Dongcheng.

sentences.zh: 孝文十二年，民有作歌歌淮南厲王曰：「一尺布，尚可縫；

translations.literal: In the twelfth year of Emperor Wen, someone among the people composed a song about King Li of Huainan, which went: 'One foot of cloth can still be sewn;
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year of Emperor Wen's reign, a popular song about King Li of Huainan began to circulate: 'A foot of cloth can still be sewn together;

sentences.zh: 一斗粟，尚可舂。

translations.literal: one peck of millet can still be hulled.
translations.idiomatic: a peck of grain can still be pounded.

sentences.zh: 兄弟二人不能相容。」

translations.literal: Two brothers cannot tolerate each other.'
translations.idiomatic: Yet two brothers cannot make room for one another.'

sentences.zh: 上聞之，乃嘆曰：「堯舜放逐骨肉，周公殺管蔡，天下稱聖。

translations.literal: When the Emperor heard this, he sighed and said: 'Yao and Shun exiled their own flesh and blood, and the Duke of Zhou killed Guan and Cai, yet the world called them sages.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor heard this song, he sighed and said, 'Yao and Shun banished their own kin, and the Duke of Zhou put Guan and Cai to death, yet all the world calls them sages.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why is this?
translations.idiomatic: Why is that?

sentences.zh: 不以私害公。

translations.literal: Because they did not let private feelings harm the public good.
translations.idiomatic: Because they did not allow private bonds to injure the public good.

sentences.zh: 天下豈以我為貪淮南王地邪？」

translations.literal: Does the realm really think that I coveted the King of Huainan's territory?'
translations.idiomatic: Does the world really believe I coveted the King of Huainan's territory?'

sentences.zh: 乃徙城陽王王淮南故地，而追尊謚淮南王為厲王，置園復如諸侯儀。

translations.literal: He then transferred the King of Chengyang to rule over the former territory of Huainan, posthumously honored the King of Huainan with the title King Li, and established a funerary park with the rites befitting a vassal king.
translations.idiomatic: He then transferred the King of Chengyang to rule the former territory of Huainan, posthumously conferred the title King Li upon the late King of Huainan, and established a funerary park with all the rites befitting a vassal king.

sentences.zh: 孝文十六年，徙淮南王喜復故城陽。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year of Emperor Wen, the King of Huainan, Xi, was transferred back to his former territory of Chengyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year of Emperor Wen's reign, the King of Huainan, Xi, was transferred back to his former territory of Chengyang.

sentences.zh: 上憐淮南厲王廢法不軌，自使失國蚤死，乃立其三子：阜陵侯安為淮南王，安陽侯勃為衡山王，陽周侯賜為廬江王，皆復得厲王時地，參分之。

translations.literal: The Emperor pitied King Li of Huainan, who had broken the law and acted improperly, bringing about the loss of his kingdom and his early death. He therefore established three of King Li's sons: the Marquis of Fuling, An, as King of Huainan; the Marquis of Anyang, Bo, as King of Hengshan; and the Marquis of Yangzhou, Ci, as King of Lujiang. All recovered the territories of King Li's time, divided into three parts.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor pitied King Li of Huainan, whose lawlessness and misconduct had brought about the loss of his kingdom and his early death. He therefore raised three of King Li's sons: An, Marquis of Fuling, to be King of Huainan; Bo, Marquis of Anyang, to be King of Hengshan; and Ci, Marquis of Yangzhou, to be King of Lujiang. The former territories of King Li were restored and divided among the three.

sentences.zh: 東城侯良前薨，無後也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Dongcheng, Liang, had previously died without an heir.
translations.idiomatic: Liang, the Marquis of Dongcheng, had died earlier without an heir.

sentences.zh: 孝景三年，吳楚七國反，吳使者至淮南，淮南王欲發兵應之。

translations.literal: In the third year of Emperor Jing, the Seven Kingdoms of Wu and Chu revolted. Envoys from Wu arrived in Huainan, and the King of Huainan wished to raise troops to join them.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Emperor Jing's reign, the Seven Kingdoms led by Wu and Chu rose in rebellion. Envoys from Wu arrived in Huainan, and the King of Huainan wanted to raise his troops in support.

sentences.zh: 其相曰：「大王必欲發兵應吳，臣願為將。」

translations.literal: His chancellor said: 'If Your Majesty truly insists on raising troops to support Wu, your subject wishes to serve as general.'
translations.idiomatic: His chancellor said, 'If Your Majesty is determined to raise troops in support of Wu, your servant asks to be appointed as their commander.'

sentences.zh: 王乃屬相兵。

translations.literal: The king then entrusted the troops to his chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The king then placed the troops under the chancellor's command.

sentences.zh: 淮南相已將兵，因城守，不聽王而為漢；

translations.literal: Once the chancellor of Huainan had taken command of the troops, he fortified the cities and held them in defense, refusing to obey the king and instead acting on behalf of the Han;
translations.idiomatic: Once the chancellor had taken command of the troops, he fortified the cities and held them in defense, disobeying the king and siding with the Han;

sentences.zh: 漢亦使曲城侯將兵救淮南：淮南以故得完。

translations.literal: the Han also sent the Marquis of Qucheng to lead troops to rescue Huainan. Huainan was thus preserved.
translations.idiomatic: the Han court also dispatched the Marquis of Qucheng with an army to relieve Huainan. In this way, the kingdom of Huainan was preserved.

sentences.zh: 吳使者至廬江，廬江王弗應，而往來使越。

translations.literal: The envoys from Wu reached Lujiang, but the King of Lujiang did not respond to them, though he did exchange envoys with Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys from Wu also reached Lujiang, but the King of Lujiang refused to join them, though he did exchange messages with the kingdom of Yue.

sentences.zh: 吳使者至衡山，衡山王堅守無二心。

translations.literal: The envoys from Wu reached Hengshan, and the King of Hengshan held firm and harbored no disloyalty.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys from Wu arrived in Hengshan as well, but the King of Hengshan stood firm and remained steadfastly loyal.

sentences.zh: 孝景四年，吳楚已破，衡山王朝，上以為貞信，乃勞苦之曰：「南方卑溼。」

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Emperor Jing, after Wu and Chu had been defeated, the King of Hengshan came to court. The Emperor considered him loyal and faithful, and consoled him, saying: 'The south is low-lying and damp.'
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Emperor Jing's reign, after the rebellion of Wu and Chu had been crushed, the King of Hengshan came to pay court. The emperor, regarding him as a man of unwavering loyalty, consoled him with the words, 'The south is low-lying and damp.'

sentences.zh: 徙衡山王王濟北，所以褒之。

translations.literal: He transferred the King of Hengshan to rule Jibei, in order to reward him.
translations.idiomatic: He transferred the King of Hengshan to the more favorable territory of Jibei as a mark of his esteem.

sentences.zh: 及薨，遂賜謚為貞王。

translations.literal: When he died, he was given the posthumous title King Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his death, he was given the posthumous title King Zhen, the Faithful.

sentences.zh: 廬江王邊越，數使使相交，故徙為衡山王，王江北。

translations.literal: The King of Lujiang bordered the territory of Yue and had frequently exchanged envoys with them. He was therefore transferred to become the King of Hengshan, ruling the territory north of the Yangtze.
translations.idiomatic: Because the King of Lujiang's territory bordered that of Yue and he had frequently exchanged envoys with them, he was transferred to become the King of Hengshan, ruling the lands north of the Yangtze.

sentences.zh: 淮南王如故。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan remained as before.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan retained his position as before.

sentences.zh: 淮南王安為人好讀書鼓琴，不喜弋獵狗馬馳騁，亦欲以行陰德拊循百姓，流譽天下。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan, An, was by nature fond of reading books and playing the zither, and did not enjoy hunting with crossbows, keeping hounds and horses, or riding at a gallop. He also wished to perform secret acts of virtue to comfort and care for the common people, and spread his reputation throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan, An, was by nature a lover of books and the zither. He took no pleasure in hunting with crossbows or racing horses and hounds. He wished instead to perform quiet acts of benevolence, to comfort the common people, and to spread his good name throughout the empire.

sentences.zh: 時時怨望厲王死，時欲畔逆，未有因也。

translations.literal: From time to time he harbored resentment over the death of King Li, and occasionally desired to rebel, but had found no opportunity.
translations.idiomatic: From time to time he brooded over the death of King Li and entertained thoughts of rebellion, but no opportunity had yet presented itself.

sentences.zh: 及建元二年，淮南王入朝。

translations.literal: In the second year of the Jianyuan era, the King of Huainan came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the Jianyuan era, the King of Huainan traveled to the capital to pay court.

sentences.zh: 素善武安侯，武安侯時為太尉，乃逆王霸上，與王語曰：「方今上無太子，大王親高皇帝孫，行仁義，天下莫不聞。

translations.literal: He had always been on good terms with the Marquis of Wu'an. The Marquis of Wu'an, who was then serving as Grand Commandant, went to meet the king at Bashang and said to him: 'At present the Emperor has no heir apparent. Your Majesty is a grandson of Emperor Gao himself, known throughout the realm for practicing benevolence and righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: He had always been on good terms with the Marquis of Wu'an, who was then serving as Grand Commandant. The marquis went to meet the king at Bashang and said, 'At present the emperor has no heir apparent. Your Majesty is a direct grandson of Emperor Gao, renowned throughout the empire for benevolence and righteousness.

sentences.zh: 即宮車一日晏駕，非大王當誰立者！」

translations.literal: Should the imperial carriage one day come to rest, who should be established if not Your Majesty!'
translations.idiomatic: Should the emperor one day pass away, who would be more fit to ascend the throne than Your Majesty!'

sentences.zh: 淮南王大喜，厚遺武安侯金財物。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan was overjoyed and lavished gold and gifts upon the Marquis of Wu'an.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was overjoyed and presented the Marquis of Wu'an with generous gifts of gold and valuables.

sentences.zh: 陰結賓客，拊循百姓，為畔逆事。

translations.literal: He secretly cultivated retainers, comforted the common people, and prepared for rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: He secretly cultivated a network of retainers, worked to win the loyalty of the common people, and began preparations for rebellion.

sentences.zh: 建元六年，彗星見，淮南王心怪之。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of the Jianyuan era, a comet appeared, and the King of Huainan was alarmed.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of the Jianyuan era, a comet appeared in the sky, and the King of Huainan took it as an ominous sign.

sentences.zh: 或說王曰：「先吳軍起時，彗星出長數尺，然尚流血千里。

translations.literal: Someone said to the king: 'When the armies of Wu rose before, a comet appeared measuring only a few feet in length, yet blood still flowed for a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Someone said to the king, 'When the armies of Wu rose in rebellion, a comet appeared spanning only a few feet, yet blood flowed for a thousand li.

sentences.zh: 今彗星長竟天，天下兵當大起。」

translations.literal: Now this comet stretches across the entire sky. Armies are sure to rise throughout the realm.'
translations.idiomatic: Now this comet stretches across the entire sky. Great armies are sure to rise throughout the empire.'

sentences.zh: 王心以為上無太子，天下有變，諸侯并爭，愈益治器械攻戰具，積金錢賂遺郡國諸侯游士奇材。

translations.literal: The king believed in his heart that since the Emperor had no heir, if a change should come over the realm and the vassal lords competed for power, he must be prepared. He redoubled his efforts to manufacture weapons and military equipment, accumulated gold and money, and bribed the commanderies, kingdoms, vassal lords, wandering scholars, and men of exceptional talent.
translations.idiomatic: In his heart, the king believed that since the emperor had no heir, should upheaval come the vassal lords would all contend for power, and he must be prepared. He redoubled his efforts to manufacture weapons and war equipment, amassed gold and treasure, and sent lavish gifts to commanderies, kingdoms, vassal lords, wandering scholars, and men of exceptional ability.

sentences.zh: 諸辨士為方略者，妄作妖言，諂諛王，王喜，多賜金錢，而謀反滋甚。

translations.literal: Various eloquent scholars and strategists recklessly produced wild talk, flattering and fawning upon the king. The king was pleased and bestowed much gold and money upon them, and the plots of rebellion grew ever more intense.
translations.idiomatic: Various glib rhetoricians and would-be strategists concocted wild claims and lavished flattery upon the king. Delighted, the king rewarded them generously with gold and money, and the conspiracies for rebellion grew ever more intense.

sentences.zh: 淮南王有女陵，慧，有口辯。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan had a daughter named Ling, who was clever and eloquent.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan had a daughter named Ling, who was intelligent and sharp-tongued.

sentences.zh: 王愛陵，常多予金錢，為中诇長安，約結上左右。

translations.literal: The king doted on Ling, constantly giving her large sums of gold and money. She served as a spy in Chang'an, establishing secret connections with those close to the Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: The king doted on Ling and showered her with gold and money. She served as his spy in Chang'an, forging secret ties with those in the emperor's inner circle.

sentences.zh: 元朔三年，上賜淮南王几杖，不朝。

translations.literal: In the third year of the Yuanshuo era, the Emperor bestowed upon the King of Huainan an armrest and a walking stick, excusing him from attending court.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of the Yuanshuo era, the emperor bestowed upon the King of Huainan a ceremonial armrest and walking stick, exempting him from the duty of attending court.

sentences.zh: 淮南王王后荼，王愛幸之。

translations.literal: The queen of the King of Huainan was named Tu, and the king loved and favored her.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan's queen was named Tu, and the king loved her dearly.

sentences.zh: 王后生太子遷，遷取王皇太后外孫修成君女為妃。

translations.literal: The queen bore the Crown Prince Qian. Qian married the daughter of the Lord of Xiucheng, who was a granddaughter of the Empress Dowager Wang, as his consort.
translations.idiomatic: The queen gave birth to the Crown Prince Qian, who was married to the daughter of the Lord of Xiucheng, a granddaughter of Empress Dowager Wang.

sentences.zh: 王謀為反具，畏太子妃知而內泄事，乃與太子謀，令詐弗愛，三月不同席。

translations.literal: The king plotted to prepare for rebellion but feared that the Crown Prince's consort would discover the plan and leak it from within. He therefore conspired with the Crown Prince, ordering him to pretend he did not love her and to not share a bed with her for three months.
translations.idiomatic: As the king laid his plans for rebellion, he feared the Crown Prince's consort might learn of the plot and betray it. He therefore conspired with the Crown Prince, instructing him to feign a loss of affection for her and to sleep apart from her for three months.

sentences.zh: 王乃詳為怒太子，閉太子使與妃同內三月，太子終不近妃。

translations.literal: The king then pretended to be angry with the Crown Prince and confined him to share quarters with the consort for three months, but the Crown Prince never once went near her.
translations.idiomatic: The king then pretended to be angry with the Crown Prince and confined them together in the same quarters for three months, but the Crown Prince never once went near his consort.

sentences.zh: 妃求去，王乃上書謝歸去之。

translations.literal: The consort asked to leave, and the king submitted a memorial apologizing and sent her back.
translations.idiomatic: The consort asked to be released from the marriage, and the king submitted a memorial of apology and sent her home.

sentences.zh: 王后荼、太子遷及女陵得愛幸王，擅國權，侵奪民田宅，妄致系人。

translations.literal: Queen Tu, Crown Prince Qian, and Princess Ling, enjoying the king's love and favor, monopolized the power of the state, seized the people's fields and homes, and had people imprisoned at will.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Tu, Crown Prince Qian, and Princess Ling, enjoying the king's favor, monopolized power in the kingdom, seized the fields and homes of the common people, and imprisoned men on baseless charges.

sentences.zh: 元朔五年，太子學用劍，自以為人莫及，聞郎中雷被巧，乃召與戲。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of the Yuanshuo era, the Crown Prince was studying swordsmanship and believed no one could match him. Hearing that the Palace Gentleman Lei Bei was skilled, he summoned him for a bout.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of the Yuanshuo era, the Crown Prince had been training in swordsmanship and believed himself without equal. Hearing that a Palace Gentleman named Lei Bei was highly skilled, he summoned him for a bout.

sentences.zh: 被一再辭讓，誤中太子。

translations.literal: Bei repeatedly yielded and held back, but accidentally struck the Crown Prince.
translations.idiomatic: Lei Bei held back again and again out of deference, but accidentally struck the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 太子怒，被恐。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince was furious, and Bei was frightened.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince flew into a rage, and Lei Bei was terrified.

sentences.zh: 此時有欲從軍者輒詣京師，被即願奮擊匈奴。

translations.literal: At this time, those who wished to join the military would go directly to the capital. Bei then volunteered to fight the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, anyone wishing to enlist could go directly to the capital. Lei Bei immediately volunteered to fight the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 太子遷數惡被於王，王使郎中令斥免，欲以禁后，被遂亡至長安，上書自明。

translations.literal: Crown Prince Qian repeatedly slandered Bei before the king. The king ordered the Prefect of the Palace Gentlemen to dismiss Bei, intending to prevent any further trouble. Bei then fled to Chang'an and submitted a memorial to clear his name.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Qian repeatedly slandered Lei Bei before the king, and the king ordered the Prefect of the Palace Gentlemen to dismiss him, intending to suppress the matter. Lei Bei then fled to Chang'an and submitted a memorial to the throne to vindicate himself.

sentences.zh: 詔下其事廷尉、河南。

translations.literal: An edict referred the matter to the Commandant of Justice and the Governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: An imperial edict referred the case to the Commandant of Justice and the Governor of Henan for investigation.

sentences.zh: 河南治，逮淮南太子，王、王后計欲無遣太子，遂發兵反，計猶豫，十餘日未定。

translations.literal: When Henan investigated and issued a summons for the Crown Prince of Huainan, the king and queen plotted not to send the Crown Prince and to raise troops in rebellion. But they hesitated, and for more than ten days the plan remained undecided.
translations.idiomatic: When the Governor of Henan launched his investigation and issued a warrant for the Crown Prince of Huainan, the king and queen plotted to refuse the summons and raise troops in open rebellion. But they vacillated, and for more than ten days the decision remained unsettled.

sentences.zh: 會有詔，即訊太子。

translations.literal: It happened that an imperial edict arrived to interrogate the Crown Prince at once.
translations.idiomatic: Before they could act, an imperial edict arrived ordering the immediate interrogation of the Crown Prince.

sentences.zh: 當是時，淮南相怒壽春丞留太子逮不遣，劾不敬。

translations.literal: At that time, the Chancellor of Huainan was furious that the Assistant Magistrate of Shouchun had detained the Crown Prince and not sent him, and impeached him for disrespect.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the Chancellor of Huainan was outraged that the Assistant Magistrate of Shouchun had detained the Crown Prince instead of sending him as ordered, and impeached the magistrate for disrespect.

sentences.zh: 王以請相，相弗聽。

translations.literal: The king petitioned the chancellor, but the chancellor refused to listen.
translations.idiomatic: The king appealed to the chancellor, but the chancellor refused to comply.

sentences.zh: 王使人上書告相，事下廷尉治。

translations.literal: The king sent someone to submit a memorial accusing the chancellor. The matter was referred to the Commandant of Justice for investigation.
translations.idiomatic: The king sent someone to submit a memorial accusing the chancellor. The case was referred to the Commandant of Justice for investigation.

sentences.zh: 蹤跡連王，王使人候伺漢公卿，公卿請逮捕治王。

translations.literal: The trail of evidence led back to the king. The king sent agents to spy on the Han ministers. The ministers requested that the king be arrested and tried.
translations.idiomatic: The trail of evidence led back to the king himself. The king sent agents to spy on the Han court officials, and the officials petitioned for the king's arrest and trial.

sentences.zh: 王恐事發，太子遷謀曰：「漢使即逮王，王令人衣衛士衣，持戟居庭中，王旁有非是，則刺殺之，臣亦使人刺殺淮南中尉，乃舉兵，未晚。」

translations.literal: The king feared the plot would be exposed. Crown Prince Qian devised a plan: 'If the Han envoys come to arrest Your Majesty, have men dressed in the uniforms of palace guards, armed with halberds, stationed in the courtyard. If anything goes wrong at your side, they will assassinate the envoys. Your servant will also have the Colonel of Huainan assassinated, and then we can raise troops. It will not be too late.'
translations.idiomatic: The king feared the conspiracy would be exposed. Crown Prince Qian proposed a plan: 'If the Han envoys come to arrest Your Majesty, station men disguised as palace guards, armed with halberds, in the courtyard. Should anything go amiss, they will cut the envoys down. I will also have the Colonel of Huainan assassinated, and then we can raise our forces. It will not be too late.'

sentences.zh: 是時上不許公卿請，而遣漢中尉宏即訊驗王。

translations.literal: At this time, the Emperor did not approve the ministers' request, but instead dispatched the Han Colonel Hong to immediately question and examine the king.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the emperor rejected the ministers' petition and instead dispatched the Han Colonel Hong to question and examine the king in person.

sentences.zh: 王聞漢使來，即如太子謀計。

translations.literal: When the king heard that the Han envoy was coming, he immediately followed the Crown Prince's plan.
translations.idiomatic: When the king learned that the Han envoy was on his way, he at once put the Crown Prince's plan into action.

sentences.zh: 漢中尉至，王視其顏色和，訊王以斥雷被事耳，王自度無何，不發。

translations.literal: When the Han Colonel arrived, the king saw that his expression was mild and that the questioning was only about the dismissal of Lei Bei. The king judged there was nothing to worry about and did not launch the attack.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han Colonel arrived, the king saw that his expression was mild and that the questioning concerned only the dismissal of Lei Bei. Judging there was nothing to fear, the king called off the attack.

sentences.zh: 中尉還，以聞。

translations.literal: The Colonel returned and reported to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel returned to the capital and reported his findings to the throne.

sentences.zh: 公卿治者曰：「淮南王安擁閼奮擊匈奴者雷被等，廢格明詔，當棄市。」

translations.literal: The ministers who investigated the case said: 'The King of Huainan, An, obstructed Lei Bei and others who wished to fight the Xiongnu, and disregarded an explicit imperial edict. He deserves public execution.'
translations.idiomatic: The ministers who had investigated the case declared, 'The King of Huainan, An, obstructed Lei Bei and others who wished to fight the Xiongnu and defied an explicit imperial edict. He deserves public execution.'

sentences.zh: 詔弗許。

translations.literal: The imperial edict did not approve.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor refused to approve the sentence.

sentences.zh: 公卿請廢勿王，詔弗許。

translations.literal: The ministers petitioned to depose him from kingship. The edict refused.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers petitioned to strip him of his kingship, but the emperor refused.

sentences.zh: 公卿請削五縣，詔削二縣。

translations.literal: The ministers requested that five counties be taken from him. The edict reduced this to two counties.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers requested that five counties be taken from him, but the emperor reduced the penalty to two counties.

sentences.zh: 使中尉宏赦淮南王罪，罰以削地。

translations.literal: The Colonel Hong was sent to pardon the King of Huainan's crimes and punish him only with the reduction of his territory.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Hong was dispatched to pardon the King of Huainan's crimes, with the sole punishment being the reduction of his territory.

sentences.zh: 中尉入淮南界，宣言赦王。

translations.literal: The Colonel entered the borders of Huainan and proclaimed the pardon of the king.
translations.idiomatic: When the Colonel entered the borders of Huainan, he proclaimed that the king had been pardoned.

sentences.zh: 王初聞漢公卿請誅之，未知得削地，聞漢使來，恐其捕之，乃與太子謀刺之如前計。

translations.literal: The king had first heard that the Han ministers had petitioned for his execution. Not knowing that the penalty had been reduced to loss of territory, and hearing that a Han envoy was approaching, he feared arrest and conspired with the Crown Prince to assassinate the envoy as before.
translations.idiomatic: The king had heard only that the Han ministers had petitioned for his execution. Unaware that the penalty had been reduced to a loss of territory, and learning that a Han envoy was approaching, he feared he was about to be arrested and conspired with the Crown Prince to carry out the assassination plan as before.

sentences.zh: 及中尉至，即賀王，王以故不發。

translations.literal: When the Colonel arrived, he immediately congratulated the king, and so the king did not launch the attack.
translations.idiomatic: When the Colonel arrived and immediately offered his congratulations on the pardon, the king abandoned the attack.

sentences.zh: 其後自傷曰：「吾行仁義見削，甚恥之。」

translations.literal: Afterward he lamented: 'I have practiced benevolence and righteousness and yet had my territory reduced. I am deeply ashamed.'
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he lamented bitterly, 'I have practiced benevolence and righteousness, and yet my territory has been stripped away. It is a profound humiliation.'

sentences.zh: 然淮南王削地之后，其為反謀益甚。

translations.literal: After the King of Huainan's territory was reduced, his plots of rebellion grew even more intense.
translations.idiomatic: After his territory was reduced, the King of Huainan's conspiracies for rebellion only intensified.

sentences.zh: 諸使道從長安來，為妄妖言，言上無男，漢不治，即喜；

translations.literal: When various envoys came from Chang'an along the road, if they spoke wildly, saying the Emperor had no male heir and the Han was in disorder, the king was delighted;
translations.idiomatic: Whenever agents returned from Chang'an bearing wild reports that the emperor had no male heir and the Han court was in disarray, the king was delighted;

sentences.zh: 即言漢廷治，有男，王怒，以為妄言，非也。

translations.literal: but if they said the Han court was well-governed and the Emperor had a male heir, the king was angry and dismissed it as falsehood.
translations.idiomatic: but whenever they reported that the Han court was well-governed and the emperor had a male heir, the king flew into a rage and dismissed their words as lies.

sentences.zh: 王日夜與伍被、左吳等案輿地圖，部署兵所從入。

translations.literal: Day and night, the king pored over maps with Wu Bei, Zuo Wu, and others, planning the routes for the advance of their troops.
translations.idiomatic: Day and night, the king pored over maps with Wu Bei, Zuo Wu, and the others, plotting the routes along which their armies would advance.

sentences.zh: 王曰：「上無太子，宮車即晏駕，廷臣必徵膠東王，不即常山王，諸侯并爭，吾可以無備乎！

translations.literal: The king said: 'The Emperor has no heir. Should the imperial carriage one day come to rest, the court ministers will surely summon the King of Jiaodong, or else the King of Changshan. The vassal lords will all contend with one another. Can I afford to be unprepared!
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'The emperor has no heir. Should he pass away, the court ministers will surely summon the King of Jiaodong, or failing that, the King of Changshan. The vassal lords will all compete for the throne. Can I afford to be unprepared!

sentences.zh: 且吾高祖孫，親行仁義，陛下遇我厚，吾能忍之；

translations.literal: Besides, I am a grandson of Emperor Gao, who personally practiced benevolence and righteousness. His Majesty treats me well, and I can endure it;
translations.idiomatic: I am a direct grandson of Emperor Gao and have practiced benevolence and righteousness. The emperor treats me generously, and I can endure serving him;

sentences.zh: 萬世之後，吾寧能北面臣事豎子乎！」

translations.literal: but after ten thousand generations, could I bear to face north and serve as a subject to some youth!'
translations.idiomatic: but when he is gone, how could I ever bow my head and serve as a subject to some callow youth!'

sentences.zh: 王坐東宮，召伍被與謀，曰：「將軍上。」

translations.literal: The king sat in the eastern palace, summoned Wu Bei to deliberate, and said: 'General, come forward.'
translations.idiomatic: The king sat in the eastern palace and summoned Wu Bei to plan with him. He said, 'General, step forward.'

sentences.zh: 被悵然曰：「上寬赦大王，王復安得此亡國之語乎！

translations.literal: Bei said with dismay: 'The Emperor has generously pardoned Your Majesty. How can Your Majesty speak such words of national ruin!
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei replied in dismay, 'The emperor has generously pardoned Your Majesty. How can you now speak such words that would bring ruin upon the kingdom!

sentences.zh: 臣聞子胥諫吳王，吳王不用，乃曰『臣今見麋鹿游姑蘇之臺也』。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that Wu Zixu remonstrated with the King of Wu, but the King of Wu would not listen, so Wu Zixu said, "I can already see the deer wandering upon the terraces of Gusu."
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that Wu Zixu remonstrated with the King of Wu, but the King of Wu refused to heed him. Whereupon Wu Zixu said, 'I can already see deer roaming the terraces of Gusu Palace.'

sentences.zh: 今臣亦見宮中生荊棘，露霑衣也。」

translations.literal: Now your servant likewise sees thorns and brambles growing in the palace, and dew soaking our garments.'
translations.idiomatic: Now I too can see thorns and brambles sprouting in the palace, and dew soaking our garments.'

sentences.zh: 王怒，系伍被父母，囚之三月。

translations.literal: The king was furious. He had Wu Bei's parents arrested and imprisoned them for three months.
translations.idiomatic: The king flew into a rage, had Wu Bei's parents arrested, and kept them imprisoned for three months.

sentences.zh: 復召曰：「將軍許寡人乎？」

translations.literal: He summoned Wu Bei again and said: 'Will the General consent to my plan?'
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Wu Bei once more and asked, 'Will the General now agree to my plan?'

sentences.zh: 被曰：「不，直來為大王畫耳。

translations.literal: Bei said: 'No. I have only come to advise Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bei said, 'No. I have come only to counsel Your Majesty.

sentences.zh: 臣聞聰者聽於無聲，明者見於未形，故聖人萬舉萬全。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the wise hear what has not yet been spoken, and the perceptive see what has not yet taken form. Therefore the sage succeeds in all ten thousand endeavors.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the wise hear what is yet unspoken, and the perceptive see what is yet unformed. This is why the sage succeeds in every undertaking.

sentences.zh: 昔文王一動而功顯于千世，列為三代，此所謂因天心以動作者也，故海內不期而隨。

translations.literal: In ancient times, King Wen acted once and his achievements were celebrated for a thousand generations, ranking among the Three Dynasties. This is what is called acting in accordance with the will of Heaven, and so all within the seas followed him without being summoned.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, King Wen took one decisive action and his achievements shone for a thousand generations, ranked among the Three Dynasties. This is what it means to act in harmony with the will of Heaven, and all the world followed him without being called upon.

sentences.zh: 此千歲之可見者。

translations.literal: This can be seen across a thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: This is the lesson of a thousand years.

sentences.zh: 夫百年之秦，近世之吳楚，亦足以喻國家之存亡矣。

translations.literal: The Qin, which endured a hundred years, and the Wu and Chu of recent times, are also sufficient to illustrate the survival or destruction of a state.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin, which lasted barely a hundred years, and the kingdoms of Wu and Chu in recent times, also serve as clear lessons in what makes a state endure or perish.

sentences.zh: 臣不敢避子胥之誅，願大王毋為吳王之聽。

translations.literal: Your servant does not dare shrink from the fate of Wu Zixu, but hopes that Your Majesty will not be as deaf as the King of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: I do not shrink from suffering the fate of Wu Zixu, but I hope Your Majesty will not prove as deaf to counsel as the King of Wu.

sentences.zh: 昔秦絕聖人之道，殺術士，燔詩書，棄禮義，尚詐力，任刑罰，轉負海之粟致之西河。

translations.literal: In former times, the Qin severed the way of the sages, killed scholars, burned the Odes and Documents, cast aside rites and righteousness, exalted deceit and force, relied on punishments, and shipped the grain from the seacoast to the western reaches of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: In the past, the Qin destroyed the way of the sages, slaughtered scholars, burned the Odes and the Documents, cast aside ritual and righteousness, exalted deceit and brute force, relied upon harsh punishments, and shipped the grain harvests from the seacoast all the way to the western reaches of the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 當是之時，男子疾耕不足於糟糠，女子紡績不足於蓋形。

translations.literal: At that time, men toiled at the plow yet could not get enough chaff and husks to eat, and women spun and wove yet could not clothe their bodies.
translations.idiomatic: In those times, men toiled at the plow and still could not feed themselves even chaff and husks, while women spun and wove day and night and still could not clothe their own bodies.

sentences.zh: 遣蒙恬筑長城，東西數千里，暴兵露師常數十萬，死者不可勝數，僵尸千里，流血頃畝，百姓力竭，欲為亂者十家而五。

translations.literal: Meng Tian was dispatched to build the Great Wall, stretching thousands of li from east to west. Troops were exposed to the elements constantly, numbering in the hundreds of thousands. The dead were beyond counting, corpses stiffened for a thousand li, blood flowed across the fields, the people's strength was exhausted, and five out of every ten households wished to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin sent Meng Tian to build the Great Wall, stretching thousands of li from east to west. Hundreds of thousands of soldiers were left exposed to the elements at all times. The dead were beyond counting, corpses lay stiff for a thousand li, and blood flowed across the fields. The people's strength was spent, and five out of every ten households longed for revolt.

sentences.zh: 又使徐福入海求神異物，還為偽辭曰：『臣見海中大神，言曰：「汝西皇之使邪？」

translations.literal: The Qin also sent Xu Fu out to sea to seek divine wonders. He returned and gave a false report, saying: "Your servant saw a great spirit in the sea, who said, 'Are you an envoy of the Emperor of the West?'"
translations.idiomatic: The Qin also sent Xu Fu out to sea in search of divine wonders. Upon his return, Xu Fu fabricated the following account: 'Your servant saw a great spirit in the sea, who asked, "Are you an envoy of the Emperor of the West?"'

sentences.zh: 臣答曰：「然。」

translations.literal: Your servant answered, "Yes."
translations.idiomatic: I answered, 'Indeed I am.'

sentences.zh: 「汝何求？」

translations.literal: "What do you seek?"
translations.idiomatic: 'What do you seek?'

sentences.zh: 曰：「願請延年益壽藥。」

translations.literal: I said, "I wish to obtain the elixir of long life and immortality."
translations.idiomatic: I replied, 'I wish to obtain the elixir of longevity and immortality.'

sentences.zh: 神曰：「汝秦王之禮薄，得觀而不得取。」

translations.literal: The spirit said, "The offerings of the King of Qin are meager. You may observe but not take."
translations.idiomatic: The spirit said, 'The offerings of the King of Qin are too meager. You may look upon the elixir but not take it.'

sentences.zh: 即從臣東南至蓬萊山，見芝成宮闕，有使者銅色而龍形，光上照天。

translations.literal: Then the spirit led your servant to the southeast, to Mount Penglai, where I saw palaces and gates made of magic fungus. There were emissaries of bronze complexion and dragon form, whose light shone upward and illuminated the heavens.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit then led me to the southeast, to Mount Penglai, where I beheld palaces and gates fashioned from divine fungus. There were emissaries with bronze skin and the form of dragons, whose radiance shone upward and illuminated the sky.

sentences.zh: 於是臣再拜問曰：「宜何資以獻？」

translations.literal: Thereupon your servant bowed twice and asked, "What should be offered as tribute?"
translations.idiomatic: I bowed twice before them and asked, 'What tribute should be offered?'

sentences.zh: 海神曰：「以令名男子若振女與百工之事，即得之矣。」』

translations.literal: The sea spirit said, "Bring fine young men and women of good name, together with artisans of all trades, and the elixir shall be yours."'
translations.idiomatic: The spirit of the sea replied, 'Bring young men and women of good families, together with craftsmen of every trade, and the elixir shall be yours.''

sentences.zh: 秦皇帝大說，遣振男女三千人，資之五穀種種百工而行。

translations.literal: The Qin Emperor was greatly pleased and dispatched three thousand young men and women, provisioning them with the five grains, seeds of every kind, and artisans of all trades, and sent them on their way.
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor was delighted and dispatched three thousand young men and women, provisioning them with the five grains, all manner of seeds, and craftsmen of every trade, and sent them on their way.

sentences.zh: 徐福得平原廣澤，止王不來。

translations.literal: Xu Fu found a broad plain and vast marshes, settled there, and declared himself king, never returning.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Fu found a broad plain with vast marshes, settled there as its ruler, and never returned.

sentences.zh: 於是百姓悲痛相思，欲為亂者十家而六。

translations.literal: Thereupon the people grieved and mourned, and six out of every ten households wished to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: The people were stricken with grief and longing, and six out of every ten households wished for revolt.

sentences.zh: 又使尉佗踰五嶺攻百越。

translations.literal: The Qin also sent Commandant Tuo to cross the Five Ridges and attack the Hundred Yue peoples.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin then sent Commandant Tuo to cross the Five Ridges and attack the Hundred Yue peoples.

sentences.zh: 尉佗知中國勞極，止王不來，使人上書，求女無夫家者三萬人，以為士卒衣補。

translations.literal: Commandant Tuo, knowing that China was exhausted, settled there as its ruler and did not return. He sent a messenger to submit a memorial requesting thirty thousand unmarried women to mend the soldiers' clothing.
translations.idiomatic: Commandant Tuo, knowing that the empire was exhausted, settled there as ruler and never returned. He sent a messenger to the capital with a memorial requesting thirty thousand unmarried women to mend the soldiers' garments.

sentences.zh: 秦皇帝可其萬五千人。

translations.literal: The Qin Emperor approved fifteen thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor approved the dispatch of fifteen thousand.

sentences.zh: 於是百姓離心瓦解，欲為亂者十家而七。

translations.literal: Thereupon the people's hearts turned against the state and fell apart like tiles, and seven out of every ten households wished to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: The hearts of the people then turned from the state and all cohesion crumbled. Seven out of every ten households wished for revolt.

sentences.zh: 客謂高皇帝曰：『時可矣。』

translations.literal: A retainer said to Emperor Gao: 'The time is ripe.'
translations.idiomatic: An adviser said to Emperor Gao, 'The time has come.'

sentences.zh: 高皇帝曰：『待之，聖人當起東南閒。』

translations.literal: Emperor Gao said, 'Wait. A sage is bound to arise in the southeast.'
translations.idiomatic: But Emperor Gao replied, 'Wait. A sage is destined to arise in the southeast.'

sentences.zh: 不一年，陳勝吳廣發矣。

translations.literal: Less than a year later, Chen Sheng and Wu Guang rose in revolt.
translations.idiomatic: Within the year, Chen Sheng and Wu Guang rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 高皇始於豐沛，一倡天下不期而響應者不可勝數也。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao began from Feng and Pei, and once he raised the call, those throughout the realm who responded without being summoned were beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao rose from the humble villages of Feng and Pei, and once he raised the call, people from every corner of the realm rallied to him without being summoned, more than could be counted.

sentences.zh: 此所謂蹈瑕候閒，因秦之亡而動者也。

translations.literal: This is what is called seizing upon a flaw and exploiting an opening, acting because of the fall of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: This is what it means to exploit a weakness and seize an opening, to ride the wave of the Qin's collapse.

sentences.zh: 百姓願之，若旱之望雨，故起於行陳之中而立為天子，功高三王，德傳無窮。

translations.literal: The people desired him as the drought desires rain, and so he rose from the ranks of the army to become the Son of Heaven. His achievements surpassed those of the Three Kings, and his virtue has been transmitted without end.
translations.idiomatic: The people longed for him as parched earth longs for rain. And so he rose from the ranks of the army to become the Son of Heaven, with achievements surpassing those of the Three Kings and a legacy of virtue that endures forever.

sentences.zh: 今大王見高皇帝得天下之易也，獨不觀近世之吳楚乎？

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty sees how easily Emperor Gao won the realm, but does Your Majesty not also consider the example of Wu and Chu in recent times?
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty sees how easily Emperor Gao won the realm, but have you not also considered the example of Wu and Chu in recent times?

sentences.zh: 夫吳王賜號為劉氏祭酒，復不朝，王四郡之眾，地方數千里，內鑄消銅以為錢，東煑海水以為鹽，上取江陵木以為船，一船之載當中國數十兩車，國富民眾。

translations.literal: The King of Wu was given the title of senior elder of the Liu clan, and he was excused from attending court. He ruled over the people of four commanderies, his territory stretching thousands of li. At home he smelted copper to mint coins, to the east he boiled seawater to make salt, and upstream he took timber from Jiangling to build ships. A single ship could carry as much as several dozen carts in the interior. His kingdom was rich and his people numerous.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wu held the honored title of eldest among the Liu clan and was exempted from attending court. He ruled four commanderies, with territory stretching thousands of li. Within his borders he smelted copper into coins, along the eastern coast he boiled seawater into salt, and from the upper Yangtze he took timber from Jiangling to build ships, each one capable of carrying the load of dozens of carts from the interior. His kingdom was wealthy and his people numerous.

sentences.zh: 行珠玉金帛賂諸侯宗室大臣，獨竇氏不與。

translations.literal: He distributed pearls, jade, gold, and silk to bribe the vassal lords, the imperial clan, and the great ministers. Only the Dou clan refused to take part.
translations.idiomatic: He distributed pearls, jade, gold, and silk as bribes to the vassal lords, members of the imperial clan, and the senior ministers. Only the Dou clan refused his gifts.

sentences.zh: 計定謀成，舉兵而西。

translations.literal: With his plans set and his schemes in place, he raised his troops and marched west.
translations.idiomatic: With his plans laid and his schemes complete, he raised his armies and marched west.

sentences.zh: 破於大梁，敗於狐父，奔走而東，至於丹徒，越人禽之，身死絕祀，為天下笑。

translations.literal: He was broken at Daliang, defeated at Hufu, and fled east until he reached Dantu, where the Yue people captured him. He died and his line was cut off, becoming a laughingstock for the entire realm.
translations.idiomatic: He was broken at Daliang, routed at Hufu, and fled eastward until he reached Dantu, where the Yue people captured him. He died, his ancestral sacrifices were ended, and he became a laughingstock for the entire empire.

sentences.zh: 夫以吳越之眾不能成功者何？

translations.literal: Why was it that with the masses of Wu and Yue he could not succeed?
translations.idiomatic: With all the multitudes of Wu and Yue at his command, why did he fail?

sentences.zh: 誠逆天道而不知時也。

translations.literal: It was truly because he defied the Way of Heaven and did not understand the times.
translations.idiomatic: Because he truly defied the Way of Heaven and failed to read the times.

sentences.zh: 方今大王之兵眾不能十分吳楚之一，天下安寧有萬倍於秦之時，願大王從臣之計。

translations.literal: At present, Your Majesty's troops number less than a tenth of Wu and Chu's, and the realm is at peace, ten thousand times more so than in the time of Qin. I beseech Your Majesty to heed my counsel.
translations.idiomatic: At present, Your Majesty's forces amount to less than a tenth of what Wu and Chu commanded, and the empire is at peace, ten-thousand-fold more stable than in the time of the Qin. I implore Your Majesty to heed my counsel.

sentences.zh: 大王不從臣之計，今見大王事必不成而語先泄也。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty does not follow my counsel, I can now foresee that Your Majesty's enterprise will surely fail and the plot will be leaked beforehand.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty will not follow my counsel, I can foresee that the enterprise will surely fail and the plot will be discovered before it can be carried out.

sentences.zh: 臣聞微子過故國而悲，於是作麥秀之歌，是痛紂之不用王子比干也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the Viscount of Wei passed through his former state and was filled with sorrow. He thereupon composed the Song of the Flourishing Wheat, grieving that Zhou did not heed Prince Bi Gan.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the Viscount of Wei passed through the ruins of his former state and was filled with sorrow, composing the 'Song of the Flourishing Wheat,' lamenting that King Zhou had not heeded Prince Bi Gan.

sentences.zh: 故孟子曰『紂貴為天子，死曾不若匹夫』。

translations.literal: Thus Mencius said, 'Though Zhou was honored as the Son of Heaven, in death he was not even the equal of a common man.'
translations.idiomatic: This is why Mencius said, 'Though Zhou held the throne of the Son of Heaven, in death he was not even the equal of a common man.'

sentences.zh: 是紂先自絕於天下久矣，非死之日而天下去之。

translations.literal: Zhou had already severed himself from the realm long before; it was not on the day of his death that the realm abandoned him.
